frame_analyses.description: Initial view of foam appendage model with pre-tied ligating loop visible at upper left of field. Two needle drivers visible in upper frame.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appendage positioned vertically in surgical field; loop pre-tied and ready for deployment

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher instrument (white/silver rod) enters field from right side of screen, approaching the ligating loop area
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth instrument entry; knot pusher properly oriented for loop deployment

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher advances toward loop; loop begins to open and slide over appendage tip
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled advancement; loop sliding smoothly onto appendage

frame_analyses.description: Loop now positioned around mid-shaft of appendage; knot pusher continues steady advancement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop appears to be advancing toward marked line on appendage

frame_analyses.description: Loop positioned approximately at or very near the marked target line on appendage
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop placement appears accurate relative to mark; positioning symmetrical

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher rod held steady at position; loop fully encircles appendage at marked line
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop tensioned; ready for cinching motion

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher applies pressure and advances further, cinching loop tightly around appendage
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Firm but controlled cinching; loop tightening concentrically around tissue

frame_analyses.description: Loop fully cinched and tightened around appendage at marked line; minimal gap visible between loop and tissue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Secure knot placement; loop appears completely tensioned

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher continues to hold loop secure; suture tail extends distally from cinched knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop remaining stable; tail positioned for cutting

frame_analyses.description: Scissors or cutting instrument (visible in right frame) approaches suture tail to cut excess material
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Tail cutting positioned distal to knot to complete task

frame_analyses.description: Final frame showing ligating loop fully deployed, cinched, and secured at marked line on appendage; tail cut
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion: loop secure at marked line, no visible gaps, appendage intact

penalties.description: Loop appears to be positioned at or within 1mm of marked line. No measurable deviation observed.

loop_deployment.notes: Loop deployed smoothly with steady knot pusher control

cinch_quality.notes: Knot fully tensioned; no visible slack in loop

confidence_rationale: Video is clearly an animated/instructional demonstration of proper endoloop technique. Frame sequence is smooth and illustrative. Loop placement at marked line is visible and accurate. Timing cannot be directly measured from frames as this is an educational animation with likely sped-up or idealized sequencing, but the completion pattern is consistent with expert execution. Loop cinching appears complete and secure. Appendage integrity maintained throughout.
cannot_determine: Actual real-time completion duration — animation timing may not reflect clinical reality
Exact mm deviation at mark — animation resolution limits precise measurement, but placement visually appears accurate
technique_summary: Expert-level demonstration of endoloop deployment technique. Loop advances smoothly over appendage, positions at marked line with excellent accuracy, cinches concentrically without gaps, and tail is cleanly cut. No complications or deviations observed. This is a teaching demonstration showing ideal form.
strengths: Smooth, controlled loop deployment with no hesitation
Accurate loop placement at marked target
Complete and secure cinching with no visible slack
Proper tail cutting technique distal to knot

demonstrated_techniques: Pre-tied ligating loop deployment over appendage tip
Steady advancement of knot pusher rod to slide loop into position
Concentric loop positioning at anatomical mark
Firm cinching motion to tighten knot fully around tissue
Controlled tail cutting distal to secured knot
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding jerky or uncontrolled loop advancement
Ensuring loop placement exactly at marked line (not proximal or distal)
Completing cinch fully to eliminate gaps
